identified_risks.description: The team may struggle to handle parallel development across multiple modules, leading to delays.
Unexpected regulatory changes could impact the migration process and require additional resources.
The migration may exceed the allocated budget of 2M USD due to unforeseen complexities.
Integration between microservices and legacy systems may lead to performance bottlenecks.

critical_assumptions.statement: No regulatory changes expected during the migration period.
The budget of 2M USD is sufficient for the entire migration.
Legacy systems will remain stable during the migration process.

failure_modes.description: Delays in development due to team overload.
failure_modes.trigger_conditions: Team is unable to manage multiple modules simultaneously.

failure_modes.description: Regulatory changes necessitate a halt or rework of the migration.
failure_modes.trigger_conditions: New regulations are introduced during the migration.

failure_modes.description: Unexpected costs arise from complexities in the migration.
failure_modes.trigger_conditions: Unforeseen technical challenges arise during the migration.

failure_modes.description: Performance issues arise from integration between microservices and legacy systems.
failure_modes.trigger_conditions: Legacy systems do not perform as expected during integration.

mitigation_ideas.suggestion: Assess team capacity and consider staggered development if necessary.
Monitor regulatory environment closely and allocate a contingency budget.
Conduct a detailed risk assessment to identify potential cost drivers early.
Plan for thorough testing and validation of integrations with legacy systems.

unknowns: Identified risks specific to the migration to microservices, including operational, regulatory, financial, and technical aspects. Assumptions were evaluated for fragility, revealing potential vulnerabilities. Failure modes were linked to specific risks to provide clarity on triggers. Mitigation ideas were tailored to address the identified risks effectively.
